title: Egyptian Mythology
description: Gods, pharaohs, and legends of ancient Egypt

cards.front: Ra — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian sun god and king of the gods; sailed across the sky each day in a solar barque

cards.front: Which Egyptian god is the sun god and king of the gods?
cards.back: Ra

cards.front: Osiris — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian god of the dead, the afterlife, and resurrection; ruler of the underworld

cards.front: Which Egyptian god rules the underworld and judges the dead?
cards.back: Osiris

cards.front: Isis — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian goddess of magic, motherhood, and healing; wife of Osiris and mother of Horus

cards.front: Which Egyptian goddess is associated with magic and motherhood, and reassembled her husband's body?
cards.back: Isis

cards.front: Horus — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian sky god depicted with a falcon head; son of Osiris and Isis, protector of the pharaoh

cards.front: Which falcon-headed Egyptian god is the protector of the pharaoh?
cards.back: Horus

cards.front: Anubis — what is he known for?
cards.back: Jackal-headed Egyptian god of embalming and mummification; guided souls to the afterlife

cards.front: Which jackal-headed Egyptian god oversaw mummification and guided souls to the afterlife?
cards.back: Anubis

cards.front: Thoth — what is he known for?
cards.back: Ibis-headed Egyptian god of wisdom, writing, and knowledge; inventor of hieroglyphics

cards.front: Which ibis-headed Egyptian god invented writing and hieroglyphics?
cards.back: Thoth

cards.front: Set (Seth) — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian god of chaos, storms, and the desert; murdered his brother Osiris

cards.front: Which Egyptian god of chaos murdered his brother Osiris?
cards.back: Set (Seth)

cards.front: Bastet — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian cat goddess of home, fertility, and protection

cards.front: Which Egyptian goddess is depicted as a cat and associated with home and protection?
cards.back: Bastet

cards.front: Hathor — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian goddess of love, beauty, music, and joy; often depicted with cow horns and a sun disk

cards.front: Which Egyptian goddess of love and music is depicted with cow horns and a sun disk?
cards.back: Hathor

cards.front: Sobek — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian crocodile-headed god of the Nile, water, and military power

cards.front: Which crocodile-headed Egyptian god was associated with the Nile?
cards.back: Sobek

cards.front: Sekhmet — what is she known for?
cards.back: Lion-headed Egyptian goddess of war, destruction, and healing

cards.front: Which lion-headed Egyptian goddess represented war and destruction?
cards.back: Sekhmet

cards.front: Ma'at — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian goddess of truth, justice, and cosmic order; her feather was used to weigh the heart of the dead

cards.front: Whose feather was used to weigh the hearts of the dead in Egyptian mythology?
cards.back: Ma'at

cards.front: Ptah — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian god of creation, craftsmen, and architects; patron of Memphis

cards.front: Which Egyptian god of creation and craftsmen was the patron deity of Memphis?
cards.back: Ptah

cards.front: Nut — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian goddess of the sky; depicted arching over the earth with stars on her body

cards.front: Which Egyptian goddess personifies the sky and is shown arching over the earth?
cards.back: Nut

cards.front: Geb — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian god of the earth; husband of Nut and father of Osiris, Isis, Set, and Nephthys

cards.front: Which Egyptian god personifies the earth and is the husband of Nut?
cards.back: Geb

cards.front: Tefnut — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian goddess of moisture, rain, and dew; twin sister and wife of Shu

cards.front: Which Egyptian goddess represents moisture and rain?
cards.back: Tefnut

cards.front: Shu — what is he known for?
cards.back: Egyptian god of air and sunlight; holds up the sky goddess Nut, separating her from Geb

cards.front: Which Egyptian god of air separates the sky (Nut) from the earth (Geb)?
cards.back: Shu

cards.front: Khnum — what is he known for?
cards.back: Ram-headed Egyptian god who molded humans from clay on his potter's wheel

cards.front: Which ram-headed Egyptian god shaped humans on a potter's wheel?
cards.back: Khnum

cards.front: Nephthys — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian goddess of mourning, the night, and protection of the dead; sister of Isis and wife of Set

cards.front: Which Egyptian goddess of mourning is the sister of Isis and wife of Set?
cards.back: Nephthys

cards.front: Ammit — what is she known for?
cards.back: Egyptian demon with the head of a crocodile, torso of a lion, and hindquarters of a hippo; devoured the hearts of the unworthy dead

cards.front: Which Egyptian creature devoured the hearts of those who failed the weighing of the heart?
cards.back: Ammit

cards.front: What is the Book of the Dead?
cards.back: A collection of ancient Egyptian spells and instructions to help the deceased navigate the afterlife

cards.front: Ancient Egyptian spells and instructions to guide the dead through the afterlife
cards.back: The Book of the Dead

cards.front: What is the weighing of the heart ceremony?
cards.back: A judgment in which the dead person's heart was weighed against Ma'at's feather of truth; a lighter heart meant a worthy soul

cards.front: In the weighing of the heart, what happened if the heart was heavier than Ma'at's feather?
cards.back: Ammit devoured the heart, and the soul ceased to exist

cards.front: What is the ankh?
cards.back: An ancient Egyptian cross-shaped symbol representing eternal life

cards.front: Which ancient Egyptian symbol shaped like a cross with a loop at the top represents eternal life?
cards.back: The ankh

cards.front: What is the Eye of Horus?
cards.back: An ancient Egyptian symbol of protection, royal power, and good health

cards.front: Which ancient Egyptian symbol of protection is associated with a falcon-headed god's restored eye?
cards.back: The Eye of Horus

cards.front: What does the scarab beetle symbolize in Egyptian mythology?
cards.back: Rebirth, transformation, and the rising sun — linked to the god Khepri

cards.front: Which Egyptian god is represented by a scarab beetle and associated with the rising sun?
cards.back: Khepri

cards.front: What are canopic jars?
cards.back: Four jars used during mummification to store and protect the deceased's lungs, liver, stomach, and intestines

cards.front: Which four organs were stored in canopic jars during Egyptian mummification?
cards.back: Lungs, liver, stomach, and intestines

cards.front: What is the Great Sphinx of Giza?
cards.back: A limestone statue with the body of a lion and the head of a human (likely Pharaoh Khafre), guarding the Giza pyramid complex

cards.front: Which ancient Egyptian monument has the body of a lion and the head of a human?
cards.back: The Great Sphinx of Giza

cards.front: Why were pharaohs mummified?
cards.back: Egyptians believed preserving the body was necessary for the soul (ka and ba) to survive in the afterlife

cards.front: What are 'ka' and 'ba' in Egyptian belief?
cards.back: Ka is the life force or spirit; ba is the personality or soul — both needed the preserved body to survive after death

cards.front: Why were Egyptian pharaohs considered gods?
cards.back: Pharaohs were believed to be the living embodiment of Horus and became Osiris after death

cards.front: Which god was the pharaoh believed to embody during his lifetime?
cards.back: Horus

cards.front: What is hieroglyphics?
cards.back: The ancient Egyptian writing system using pictorial symbols; used on temples, tombs, and papyrus

cards.front: Ancient Egyptian writing system using pictorial symbols on temples and tombs
cards.back: Hieroglyphics

cards.front: Who murdered Osiris in Egyptian mythology?
cards.back: His brother Set (Seth), who trapped him in a coffin and scattered his body parts

cards.front: What did Isis do after Set murdered Osiris?
cards.back: She gathered and reassembled Osiris's scattered body parts, then used magic to resurrect him

cards.front: Who fought Set to avenge his father Osiris and claim the throne of Egypt?
cards.back: Horus

cards.front: What is Duat in Egyptian mythology?
cards.back: The Egyptian underworld — the realm of the dead where souls were judged

cards.front: The Egyptian underworld where souls traveled to be judged
cards.back: Duat
